button.name: Button
button.description: Primary interactive element for triggering actions. Use for form submissions, confirmations, and navigation.

loading.description: Shows a loading spinner and disables interaction

constraints: Always use an approved variant; never use arbitrary background colors on buttons
Use the 'destructive' variant only for irreversible actions (delete, remove)
A loading button must disable all interaction and show a spinner
Minimum touch target is 44×44px to meet WCAG accessibility requirements
Button labels should be sentence case (e.g. 'Save changes', not 'SAVE CHANGES')
Do not use more than one primary button per section or form
Icon-only buttons must include an aria-label describing the action

anatomy.compositionNotes: Icon children should use the Icon component. Label text is required; icon-only buttons must provide aria-label.

variantGuidance.primary: Default action; use for the single most important action per page section. Do not use more than one per form or section.
variantGuidance.secondary: Supporting actions; use alongside a primary button for secondary choices (e.g. Cancel next to Submit).
variantGuidance.ghost: Tertiary or low-emphasis actions; use in toolbars, cards, or anywhere visual weight should be minimal.
variantGuidance.destructive: Irreversible destructive actions only (delete, remove, revoke). Always pair with a confirmation dialog.

input.name: Input
input.description: Single-line text input for collecting user data. Always pair with a visible label.

errorMessage.description: Validation error shown when variant is 'error'

placeholder.description: Hint text inside the field — not a substitute for a label

constraints: Every input must have a visible label — do not rely on placeholder text alone
Error messages must be linked to the input via aria-describedby
Do not use placeholder text as a substitute for a label
Input borders must achieve 3:1 contrast ratio against the background (WCAG 1.4.11)
Display error state and errorMessage together — never show one without the other
Helper text and error messages must not overlap; show only the error when invalid

ariaRequired: aria-describedby pointing to helperText or errorMessage

slots.label: Required visible label element (label[for])
slots.helperText: Optional guidance text below the field
slots.leadingIcon: Optional icon inside the left edge of the field
slots.trailingIcon: Optional icon inside the right edge of the field

anatomy.compositionNotes: Always pair with a visible label. Error and helper text should not be shown simultaneously.

variantGuidance.default: Standard bordered input; use for most free-text form fields.
variantGuidance.filled: Filled/shaded background variant; use on light surfaces where more visual weight is needed.
variantGuidance.outline: Outlined variant with prominent border; use when the field needs to stand out on a dense layout.
variantGuidance.error: Set automatically via state='error'; do not set variant='error' directly — use state prop.

card.name: Card
card.description: Container for grouping related content into a visually distinct surface.

interactive.description: Adds hover/focus states for clickable cards (e.g. link cards)

constraints: Do not nest interactive cards inside other interactive cards
Elevated cards should not be used on dark or colored backgrounds without a token adjustment
Internal card content must use the established spacing scale — no arbitrary padding values
If a card is entirely clickable, wrap it in an anchor element and set an appropriate aria-label

accessibility.interactiveNote: When interactive=true the card must be keyboard-focusable and have a visible focus ring
ariaRequired: aria-label when interactive and no visible heading present

slots.header: Optional card header — title and optional actions
slots.media: Optional image or media area at the top of the card

anatomy.compositionNotes: Cards are layout containers. The footer slot is the canonical place for action buttons. Do not nest cards.

variantGuidance.default: Flat white card with border; use for content grouping on white or light-gray backgrounds.
variantGuidance.elevated: Card with shadow; use when the card needs to appear above the page surface (e.g. on colored backgrounds).
variantGuidance.outline: Card with only a border, no shadow; use in dense layouts or on white backgrounds where shadow looks heavy.
variantGuidance.interactive: Clickable card variant with hover/focus states; use when the entire card is a navigation target.

badge.name: Badge
badge.description: Small label used to convey status, counts, or categorical metadata. Purely informational — not interactive.

dot.description: Renders a colored dot instead of text — useful for status indicators

constraints: Do not use a badge as an interactive element — use a button or tag component instead
Badge text should be 1–3 words maximum; longer strings break the visual compact form
Never use arbitrary colors; only approved variants are permitted
Dot badges must include a visually hidden text label for screen readers

ariaRequired: aria-label or visually hidden text when using dot variant

anatomy.compositionNotes: Badge is an inline element. When used to indicate status on another component, position it as a child of that component's overlay slot.

variantGuidance.default: Neutral/gray; use for counts, labels, or informational metadata.
variantGuidance.success: Green; use for completed, active, or positive status.
variantGuidance.warning: Yellow/amber; use for pending, at-risk, or cautionary status.
variantGuidance.error: Red; use for failed, blocked, or critical status.
variantGuidance.info: Blue; use for informational or in-progress status.

modal.name: Modal
modal.description: Dialog overlay that interrupts the current flow to request a decision or display critical information. Use sparingly — prefer inline patterns when possible.

onClose.description: Callback invoked when the modal requests to close

title.description: Heading displayed in the modal header

closeOnOverlayClick.description: Dismiss the modal when clicking the backdrop overlay

constraints: Every modal must have a descriptive title visible in the header
The alert variant is reserved for destructive or irreversible confirmations only
Focus must be trapped inside the modal while it is open
Pressing Escape must close non-alert modals
Scroll must be locked on the document body while a modal is open
Do not stack more than two modals — prefer wizards or multi-step flows instead
fullscreen variant should only be used on mobile or for immersive content

ariaRequired: aria-labelledby pointing to the modal title element
aria-describedby pointing to the modal body when content is descriptive
accessibility.focusBehavior: Move focus to first focusable element on open; return focus to trigger element on close
keyboardSupport: Tab / Shift+Tab to cycle within modal

slots.header: Title bar — title text and optional close button

anatomy.compositionNotes: Focus must be trapped inside the modal while open. The first interactive element receives focus on open. Escape key closes.

variantGuidance.default: Standard dialog modal; use for confirmations, forms, and detail views that require full user attention.
variantGuidance.alert: Destructive or critical alert; use for irreversible confirmations. Smaller, no close icon.
variantGuidance.fullscreen: Full-screen modal on mobile; use for complex flows that need maximum screen real estate.

select.name: Select
select.description: Dropdown select input for choosing one option from a list. Use when there are five or more options; prefer radio buttons for fewer choices.

errorMessage.description: Validation error shown when variant is 'error'

options.type: Array<{ value: string; label: string; disabled?: boolean }>

onChange.description: Callback with new value on change

constraints: Every select must have a visible label — do not rely on placeholder alone
Error messages must be linked to the control via aria-describedby
Do not use select for binary yes/no choices — use a checkbox or toggle instead
Option lists longer than 10 items should consider a searchable combobox instead
Display error state and errorMessage together — never show one without the other

ariaRequired: aria-describedby pointing to helperText or errorMessage
keyboardSupport: Home / End to jump to first / last option

slots.label: Required visible label element
slots.icon: Trailing chevron icon in the trigger
slots.helperText: Optional guidance text below the trigger
slots.errorMessage: Error text shown in error state

anatomy.compositionNotes: Always provide a visible label. For multi-select, add aria-multiselectable='true' on the listbox.

variantGuidance.default: Standard bordered select; use for most option-selection scenarios.
variantGuidance.filled: Filled background variant; matches the Input filled variant.

checkbox.name: Checkbox
checkbox.description: Binary or tri-state input for selecting one or multiple items from a list. Always pair with a visible label.

indeterminate.description: Tri-state indeterminate visual — used for parent checkboxes in a group

label.description: Visible label text beside the checkbox

errorMessage.description: Validation error shown when variant is 'error'

onChange.description: Callback with new checked value on change

constraints: Always provide a visible label — a standalone checkbox is inaccessible
Use indeterminate only for parent checkboxes that partially select a child group
Disabled checkboxes must not be the only way to convey required state
Group related checkboxes inside a fieldset with a legend
Error state and errorMessage must appear together

ariaRequired: aria-describedby pointing to helperText or errorMessage

anatomy.compositionNotes: The label element wraps both input and label text for accessible click area. For checkbox groups, use role='group' with a fieldset/legend.

variantGuidance.default: Standard checkbox; use for binary on/off choices or multi-select within a group.
variantGuidance.indeterminate: Partially-checked state; use when a parent checkbox represents a group where some (but not all) children are selected.

toast.name: Toast
toast.description: Transient notification that appears at the edge of the screen to communicate feedback without interrupting the user's flow.

duration.description: Auto-dismiss delay in milliseconds; 0 disables auto-dismiss

dismissible.description: Renders a close button for manual dismissal

constraints: Toasts must not contain critical information the user cannot recover if missed
Use error toasts sparingly — prefer inline form errors for field-level issues
Auto-dismiss duration must be at least 4000ms to meet WCAG 2.2.1 timing requirements
Toasts with action links must not auto-dismiss while the action is still relevant
Do not stack more than three toasts simultaneously; queue additional ones

ariaRequired: role='alert' for error/warning (polite interrupt) or role='status' for info/success
aria-live='assertive' for error, aria-live='polite' for info and success

anatomy.compositionNotes: Use role='alert' for error/warning toasts (assertive) and role='status' for success/info (polite). Render toasts in a fixed portal at the bottom/top of the viewport.

variantGuidance.success: Confirmed completion of a user-initiated action (saved, sent, created).
variantGuidance.error: Failure of a user-initiated action. Use role='alert'. Include a retry action where possible.
variantGuidance.warning: Non-blocking issue that needs attention but doesn't block the user.
variantGuidance.info: Neutral informational message (e.g. 'Your session will expire in 5 minutes').

navigation.name: Navigation
navigation.description: Top navigation bar providing site-wide wayfinding, branding, and primary actions.

logo.description: Brand logo or wordmark rendered on the left side

items.type: Array<{ label: string; href: string; icon?: ReactNode }>

activeItem.description: href value of the currently active navigation item

rightContent.description: Slot for right-aligned content such as user avatar or CTA button

constraints: Do not add more than seven primary navigation items — use mega-menus or drawers for deep structures
The logo must link to the home page and carry an aria-label describing the site name
Active item must be visually distinct through color and an accessible indicator, not color alone
transparent variant must ensure sufficient contrast with page hero imagery below it
Navigation must remain accessible at 200% browser zoom

ariaRequired: aria-label='Main navigation' on the nav element
aria-current='page' on the active item link

slots.logo: Brand logo/wordmark at start of nav

anatomy.compositionNotes: The nav element must have an accessible aria-label. Active items use aria-current='page'.

variantGuidance.horizontal: Top navigation bar; use for primary global navigation in desktop layouts.
variantGuidance.vertical: Side navigation; use for app-level navigation in dashboard or admin layouts.
variantGuidance.mobile: Full-screen mobile navigation overlay; use below the mobile breakpoint.

table.name: Table
table.description: Data table for displaying structured, comparative information in rows and columns.

columns.type: Array<{ key: string; header: string; sortable?: boolean; width?: string }>
columns.description: Column definitions including header label and optional sort config

data.description: Array of row data objects keyed to column keys

sortKey.description: Key of the currently sorted column

onSort.description: Callback with (key, direction) when a sortable column header is clicked

constraints: Tables must not be used for layout — only for tabular data with logical row/column relationships
Every column must have a non-empty header label
Sortable columns must indicate sort direction with both a visual icon and aria attributes
Tables wider than their container must scroll horizontally, not clip content
Use the empty state slot when data is an empty array

ariaRequired: aria-sort='ascending' or 'descending' on the active sort column header
aria-label or caption describing the table content
aria-live region to announce sort changes to screen readers
keyboardSupport: Enter / Space to toggle sort on focused column header

slots.caption: Accessible table title (visually hidden or visible)
slots.emptyState: Shown when tbody has no rows
slots.paginationBar: Optional pagination controls below the table

anatomy.compositionNotes: Wrap table in a scrollable container for overflow on small screens. Use th[scope='col'] for column headers and th[scope='row'] for row headers.

variantGuidance.default: Standard table with borders and alternating row backgrounds.
variantGuidance.striped: Alternating row background colors for readability in dense tables.
variantGuidance.compact: Reduced row height and padding; use when displaying many rows in limited vertical space.
variantGuidance.bordered: All-cell borders; use for financial or data-heavy tables requiring clear cell delineation.

form.name: Form
form.description: Layout wrapper that organises form controls into a consistent vertical or horizontal structure. References Button, Input, Checkbox, and Select components for field composition.

onSubmit.description: Callback invoked with form data on valid submission

layout.description: Controls label placement relative to inputs

labelWidth.description: Fixed label column width used in horizontal layout

constraints: Always include at least one Button with type='submit' inside a Form
All fields within a Form must follow the label requirements of their respective components
Use horizontal layout only for simple forms with short labels and short inputs
Do not place a Form inside another Form — nested forms are invalid HTML
Provide an accessible name for the form via aria-label or an associated heading

ariaRequired: aria-label or aria-labelledby linking to a visible form heading
novalidate attribute to use custom validation messaging
aria-describedby on the form element when global form errors are shown
keyboardSupport: Enter within a text input submits single-field forms

slots.fieldset: Optional logical grouping of related fields (fieldset/legend)
slots.field: Individual form fields via FormField wrapper
slots.actions: Form submit/cancel button row at the bottom

anatomy.compositionNotes: Use FormField as the wrapper for each field — it pairs the label, input, helper text, and error message. The actions slot should have the primary submit button on the right.

variantGuidance.default: Standard vertical stacked form; all fields in a single column.
variantGuidance.inline: Horizontal form where label and field appear on the same line; use for compact filter bars.
variantGuidance.wizard: Multi-step form; use for complex flows split across multiple screens.
